one_time_description: Deferred revenue adjustment of $15,497,123 in prior year (2022) related to Halcistat Reconciliation funding recognition; prior period restatement of government business investments reducing accumulated surplus by $1,406,207
economic_notes: Band operates Shearwater Resort, Shearwater Fuel, Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP (25%), Heiltsuk Holdings Group, Bella Bella Fuel Co., Bella Bella Band Store, Bella Bella Fisheries, and Heiltsuk Environmental Services. Fish plant upgrade funded through Heiltsuk Fishery Resource LP. Sawmill acquisition previously funded through Heiltsuk Coastal Forest Products LP. On-reserve housing loan guarantee of $2,956,562 outstanding.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to Heiltsuk Holdings Limited Partnership and Heiltsuk Holdings Ltd. fiscal 2023 financial information not being subject to audit verification at statement date; investment value of $6,945,085 and loss of $874,149 unaudited. Prior period restatement in Note 19 reduced 2022 accumulated surplus by $1,406,207 due to previously excluded government business results. Large deferred revenue balance of $57.6M primarily related to Halcistat Reconciliation funding ($37.2M) and ISC ($11.2M). 8 entities accounted for on modified equity basis as GBEs/GBPs. Total long-term debt includes loans payable $540,728 (non-current portion) plus $3,712,348 callable/current, and CMHC mortgages $1,414,298. osr calculated as total revenue minus ISC, CMHC, Province of BC, BC Housing, DFO, GoC, FNESC, BC FN Gaming, Great Bear Initiative, Coast Conservation Endowment government transfers. BC Housing $551,381 included in government transfers. No remuneration schedule present in document.
